title: ecc: magic number 91 for P-256 SPKI DER size unexplained
description: In src/crypto/ecc.rs, the literal 91 appears as the buffer size and initial out_len for public_key_der, and again in the doc comment for ecdh ('must be the 91-byte DER SubjectPublicKeyInfo'). There is no comment explaining where 91 comes from (it is the exact DER-encoded size of a P-256 SubjectPublicKeyInfo: 9-byte header + 1-byte tag + 65-byte uncompressed point). A contributor who changes this to a round number or who adds P-384 support will silently truncate or reject valid keys. Add a comment deriving the value.
